claim: As context fills up, models change behavior: they wrap up conversations early, rush through steps, and declare work done prematurely (context anxiety)
actionable: Implement context resets between agent steps with fresh context windows; use context compaction to preserve key state; monitor for premature completion
source_title: Anthropic Just Dropped the New Blueprint for Long-Running AI Agents

claim: Self-evaluation by agents is unreliable; agents praise mediocre output; separate evaluator agents using adversarial architecture (GAN-style) improves quality
actionable: Create dedicated evaluator agents with system prompts focused on skepticism; use rubric-based grading; let evaluators interact with outputs via tools
source_title: Anthropic Just Dropped the New Blueprint for Long-Running AI Agents

claim: Making subjective qualities gradable (design principles, originality, craft, functionality) enables evaluator agents to score objectively
actionable: Define explicit grading criteria for subjective work; break criteria into measurable rubrics; weight criteria based on model strengths
source_title: Anthropic Just Dropped the New Blueprint for Long-Running AI Agents

claim: Evaluator agents need tool access (Playwright MCP) to interact with outputs like real users; superficial testing misses edge cases
actionable: Wire evaluators with browser/interaction tools; have them navigate, screenshot, test edge cases; don't rely on code inspection alone
source_title: Anthropic Just Dropped the New Blueprint for Long-Running AI Agents

claim: Context resets were needed for Sonnet 4.5 but not for Opus 4.5; Opus 4.6 with context compaction alone solves context anxiety without explicit resets
actionable: Evaluate which models suffer context anxiety; use context resets for weaker models; rely on context compaction for newer stronger models
source_title: Anthropic Just Dropped the New Blueprint for Long-Running AI Agents

claim: Planner agents that expand vague specs into detailed sprints prevent underscoping; contract negotiation between generator and evaluator ensures clear definition of done
actionable: Use planner to expand requirements; split into sprints with clear definitions of done; generator and evaluator agree upfront; prevent scope creep mid-build
source_title: Anthropic Just Dropped the New Blueprint for Long-Running AI Agents

claim: Harness design is as important as the model itself; harness assumptions go stale as models improve; continuously refine harness components
actionable: Treat harness design as a first-class concern; audit harness components for outdated assumptions; simplify harness when model capabilities improve
source_title: Anthropic Just Dropped the New Blueprint for Long-Running AI Agents

claim: Building a 4-hour DAW in browser cost $125 and required planner, generator, evaluator agents with Opus 4.6 and context compaction
actionable: Use planner→generator→evaluator architecture for complex 4+ hour tasks; rely on stronger models (Opus 4.6) with context compaction to avoid resets
source_title: Anthropic Just Dropped the New Blueprint for Long-Running AI Agents
